page: Microsoft Azure DC4eds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance belonging to the DCEDV5 server family. It is built on an Intel processor architecture and features 4 dedicated vCPUs. The virtualization layer is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This server configuration includes 108 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage options for expanded disk capabilities. It does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning external networking must be configured separately. There are no integrated graphics processing units on this instance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC4eds_v5 balances dedicated compute resources with a moderate memory allocation and local storage. This resource density makes it appropriate for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution without the overhead of massive memory arrays. Suitable workloads include general-purpose applications, small-scale databases, web serving, and development environments that benefit from local disk storage.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC4eds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DCEDV5 family, powered by an Intel processor and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. This instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is supported by 108 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. It does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the server offers a balanced hardware profile, combining dedicated compute cores with local storage. This configuration is designed for general-purpose workloads, small database instances, and web serving applications that require dedicated CPU resources and local disk space without the need for massive memory footprints or specialized hardware accelerators.
